purpose: Disease-agnostic deterministic retrieval layer for a clinical AI eval-designer app. Every source here is a public API reachable from plain Python (requests). Each returns a stable, third-party-resolvable identifier = the verifiability anchor. Subscription sources (Cochrane/Embase/PsycINFO/WoS/Scopus) are intentionally EXCLUDED because they cannot live in a free, deterministic, no-auth layer.

determinism_contract.reproducible_not_bit_identical: Live DBs update, so re-running may add rows. Determinism is achieved by (1) pinned query strings, (2) explicit sort key + hard result cap (never rely on default relevance rank), (3) snapshotting raw records + retrieval timestamp into the bundle. Verifiability = re-resolvable IDs, not re-runnable ranking.
determinism_contract.verification_pass: After retrieval, resolve every captured identifier (PMID via E-utils, DOI via Crossref, NCT via CT.gov, K/DEN/PMA via openFDA). A record whose ID does not resolve is dropped, not synthesized.

sources.name: PubMed E-utilities (esearch)
sources.notes: Backbone literature index. Pair with esummary/efetch for abstracts. Add contact email param if available; not required.

sources.name: PubMed E-utilities (esummary/efetch)
sources.notes: efetch (rettype=abstract) for abstract text used in synthesis grounding.

sources.name: Europe PMC REST
sources.notes: STRONG ADD: one API unifies PubMed + Agricola + preprints + patents, returns abstract + all three IDs in one call, and full-text for OA. Reduces the number of separate calls and cross-maps IDs for free.

sources.name: NCBI MeSH (E-utils, db=mesh)
sources.notes: The disease-agnostic query-construction key. Map free-text user input -> controlled MeSH terms deterministically, so build_queries is reproducible instead of ad-hoc keyword guessing.

sources.name: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2
sources.notes: Use nctId + briefTitle (officialTitle often missing). designModule.enrollmentInfo.count for sample-size grounding.

sources.name: WHO ICTRP (global trials)
sources.notes: Global registry meta-search for multi-jurisdiction coverage. Less clean than CT.gov; enable only if non-US trials matter. [REVIEW: deferred/skipped for now — flaky API.]

sources.name: openFDA device classification
sources.notes: Product code, device_class, regulation_number, definition. Start here to find the code, then pivot to 510k/pma.

sources.name: openFDA device 510(k) [De Novo indexed here]
sources.notes: De Novo grants live in this endpoint (k_number prefix DEN), NOT a separate de_novo endpoint.

sources.name: openFDA device PMA
sources.notes: MISSING FROM CURRENT PIPELINE. Class III / high-risk pathway. Needed for disease-agnostic (some conditions => Class III).

sources.name: openFDA device adverse events (MAUDE)
sources.notes: MISSING. Real-world safety-signal grounding for the monitoring field.

sources.name: openFDA device recalls
sources.notes: MISSING. Recall precedent for risk framing.

sources.name: openFDA Drugs@FDA
sources.notes: Enable when intervention is a drug/biologic (disease-agnostic toggle). Approvals + labels.

sources.name: openFDA drug labeling (SPL)
sources.notes: Indications, endpoints, warnings from the official label.

sources.name: medRxiv/bioRxiv API
sources.notes: Recency/publication-bias coverage. Note: date-window API, not free-text; pair with Europe PMC preprint filter for keyword search. [REVIEW: skipped direct — use Europe PMC preprint filter instead.]

sources.name: Crossref
sources.notes: VERIFICATION PASS. Confirm every DOI resolves to the intended title before it enters the spec. Currently missing from the pipeline diagram.

construct_behavioral: NOTE: PubMed under-covers psychology; PsycINFO/CINAHL are subscription [C] and cannot be in the deterministic layer. Flag to user for manual retrieval; do NOT silently omit.
jurisdiction_multi_market: NOTE: EMA/EUDAMED/MHRA/PMDA/TGA lack clean free APIs; treat as [C] manual or scoped-out.
